meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Stuck with Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Break through your development plateau by mastering SaaS pricing models. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first project.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists and start architecting value. The secret to escaping beginner status isn't more code; it's understanding how to monetize features. By implementing a tiered subscription model, you transform a hobby project into a real SaaS product, forcing you to learn authentication, database management, and Stripe integration.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks mastering syntax, yet your portfolio feels like a graveyard of abandoned tutorials. The 'tutorial hell' loop is real, and it’s usually because your projects lack the gravity of a real-world business constraint. You aren't stuck because you can't code; you're stuck because you aren't solving problems that people pay to fix.

Today, we pivot. We are moving from 'code-along' to 'architect-as-a-service.' By integrating a professional SaaS pricing engine, you force your application to handle identity, billing, and access control. This is the bridge between a student project and a career-defining portfolio piece.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Establish a Free, Pro, and Enterprise tier structure to force logical separation of app features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS application with three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise. Define which features (e.g., API rate limits, storage capacity, custom domains) belong to each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate database schema for tiered subscription models using Prisma'

steps.title: Setup Stripe Integration
steps.description: Integrate Stripe billing to handle secure transactions, turning your app into a functional SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft an integration flow for Stripe checkout sessions that maps specific subscription IDs to user account tiers in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Stripe Checkout webhook handler for subscription updates'

steps.title: Implement Middleware Access Control
steps.description: Create server-side logic that restricts access based on the user's active subscription plan.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks the user's subscription status from the database and restricts access to 'Pro' routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Apply auth middleware to protect API routes based on subscription tier'

steps.title: Construct the Pricing Table UI
steps.description: Build a responsive pricing table that dynamically reflects your tiered feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React component for a pricing page that highlights the 'Pro' plan and fetches current pricing data from the backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a mobile-responsive pricing table component in Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Add Subscription Usage Limits
steps.description: Implement logic to track usage (e.g., API calls) and block users who exceed their tier limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a rate-limiter that checks the user's tier and throws a 403 error if the daily limit is exceeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add Redis-based rate limiting per user subscription tier'

pro_tips: Always build your pricing table as a separate service so it can be updated without redeploying the core application logic.
Use 'Feature Flags' instead of hardcoding tier checks; it allows you to test premium features for specific users instantly.
Never store currency values as floats; always use integer cents to avoid precision errors common in financial calculations.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database to prevent immediate service disruption when a credit card fails a payment.
Treat your pricing page as a conversion funnel; use A/B testing on your call-to-action buttons to see what drives more signups.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to a staging environment, run a load test on your subscription middleware, and invite 5 peers to test your upgrade path.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder. The gap between your current project and a professional SaaS is just a few architectural decisions away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
